framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Public IC die imaging and silicon reverse engineering
paths.what_it_is: Delidding, imaging, and analyzing the internal structure of integrated circuits, then publishing detailed write-ups or high-resolution die photography explaining how the silicon actually works.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves a candidate can read silicon at the transistor and process-node level, a rare, hard-to-fake depth of understanding that goes far beyond datasheet-level knowledge.

paths.what_it_is: Designing a hardware product (board, radio, computer) in the open, publishing schematics/PCB layouts/firmware, and sustaining the project through community adoption and iteration.
paths.why_it_signals: It demonstrates the full engineering loop, design, manufacturing, documentation, and support, done publicly where the work can be independently scrutinized and reused.

paths.path: Rigorous public teardown and board-level reverse engineering
paths.what_it_is: Publicly tracing schematics from bare boards, diagnosing failures at the component level, and documenting the reverse-engineered circuit behavior of consumer and industrial electronics.
paths.why_it_signals: It shows fast, disciplined circuit-level diagnosis under real-world constraints, the same skill that separates engineers who can debug a misbehaving board from those who can only design one in isolation.
